refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions), enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and compare against customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and teleoperation control logic.
Dependency on network link quality and safety alert timing.
coupling_check.notes: Adaptive protocol logically encapsulates communication quality management, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required QoS features.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions impacting teleoperation responsiveness.
Safety criticality demands robust error handling to avoid control loss.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding explicit FR for latency control to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation control and communication management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality status and telemetry data to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and task instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on communication protocol specifics.
Primarily physical hardware, less suitable for communication protocol logic.
Highest containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication.
Focuses on safety alerts, not communication protocol management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP to Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of interface complexity if communication quality data must be shared with Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking to mitigate diffusion risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data sharing may cause cross-module dependencies.
Retransmission logic may impact latency and safety-critical timing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module is the best fit for DP containment, minimizing diffusion compared to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of communication quality responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add latency control FR to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Clarify interface definitions between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution for telemetry and tasking.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding latency control and clarifying interfaces will improve design robustness and verification clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: To include latency control as a complementary requirement to packet loss for teleoperation communication quality.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can implement adaptive communication protocols.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Teleoperation and other modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss and latency under adaptive protocol.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution.
